_comment_1: PROJECT TIER — copy to <project>/.claude/settings.json in every Builder repo (the org-template repo should ship this by default, per Builder Flow Step 3: Provisioning). Checked into version control, reviewed in PRs, editable by the Builder but visible to everyone. This tier implements the 'Ask' and 'Monitor' rows of company-agent-guardrails' Recommended Default Stance — the 'Deny' row belongs at the managed tier (see managed-settings.example.json) so it can't be locally removed.
_comment_2: Verify exact key/glob syntax against https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions before relying on this in production.
_comment_3: Monitor tier (all shell execution, file writes, external API calls) is not a blocking mechanism — it's satisfied by the audit-logging skill + Claude Code's own session transcript. No settings.json entry needed; see docs/incident-response.md and keshet-builder-skills/audit-logging/SKILL.md for what must be logged and where.
_comment_4: The matcher above MUST include 'PowerShell', not just 'Bash' -- Claude Code exposes a distinct PowerShell tool on native Windows sessions, and pre_tool_use_guard.py has separate pattern coverage for it. A matcher of 'Bash|mcp__*' alone silently never invokes this hook on Windows PowerShell tool calls, which defeats the guard entirely on this org's primary platform.
_comment_5: The 'python3' command is a Linux/macOS convention -- stock Windows does not reliably have a 'python3' alias on PATH (only 'python' or the 'py' launcher, depending on how Python was installed). Before deploying to Windows Builder machines: verify 'python3 --version' resolves in the same shell Claude Code's hook subprocess uses, or replace this with the working interpreter invocation for your fleet (e.g. 'py -3 .claude/hooks/pre_tool_use_guard.py' or an absolute path to python.exe). Do not assume this line works as-is on a fresh Windows install -- test it first.
_comment_6: The PostToolUse compressor (post_tool_use_compressor.py + the .claude/compression/ package, both copied from enforcement/) is OPT-IN per project: shipping this settings block IS the opt-in. Matcher is 'Bash' only by design — all MVP parsers are Bash-command parsers, and a narrower matcher means fewer Python startups (latency). Same python3-on-Windows caveat as _comment_5 applies. To disable per-command: include the word NOCOMPRESS in the command. To disable machine-wide: set KESHET_NOCOMPRESS=1.
